signals.summary: This comes as the foundation continues to review its ties to the late sex offender Jeffery Epstein.

signals.title: Virginia defines agrivoltaics, expanding opportunities for solar
signals.summary: Agrivoltaic projects must “ensure flexibility for farmers to adapt to market conditions and support operational needs,” according to the law.

signals.title: PJM opposes waiver for $2B gas-fired plant in fast-track interconnection review
signals.summary: Gas turbine backlogs prompted Advanced Power Services to seek changes to its project in PJM’s fast-track Reliability Resource Initiative interconnection review, but the grid operator said granting such a waiver would be unfair to other developers.

signals.title: Utilities are not spending enough on low-income efficiency: ACEEE
signals.summary: Energy efficiency becomes more important as prices rise because it is “one of the only direct levers” a household has to reduce their bills, said Anna Johnson, senior policy manager at the American Council for an Energy-Efficient Economy.

signals.title: Here's how Trump Accounts could affect women's retirement savings gap
signals.summary: While Trump Accounts are unlikely to directly improve retirement savings among women, there may be an indirect benefit, says one expert.

signals.title: Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now
signals.summary: The ruling by the Supreme Court means that Lisa Cook will remain as a Federal Reserve governor as her lawsuit challenging Trump's effort to fire her proceeds.

signals.title: Trump laments 'tremendous loss' on mail-in ballots at Supreme Court, doubles down on voter-ID bill
signals.summary: Justice Amy Coney Barrett, whom Trump nominated in 2020, rejected arguments that federal laws preempt Mississippi permitting late-arriving absentee ballots.

signals.title: Short sellers keep betting against Pop Mart — even though it's been a losing trade
signals.summary: Short sellers are doubling down on Pop Mart International even as a recent share price recovery turns their bearish bets on the Chinese toymaker increasingly risky.

signals.title: Japanese yen sinks to 40-year low, keeping intervention risks in focus
signals.summary: The Japanese yen weakened to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar since 1986 on Tuesday, keeping investors on alert for possible intervention from Japanese authorities.

signals.title: Japan is raising visa fees by up to 400% for the first time in nearly 50 years. Here's why
signals.summary: A single entry visa will climb to 15,000 yen, from the 3,000 yen charged currently, while multiple entry visas will cost 30,000 yen, up from the 6,000 yen now.
